failures.summary: Sandbox setup failure leaves Slack with only an error-state plan/Thinking block while DB/SSE has no replayable terminal event.
expected_assertions: pre-ready sandbox failures insert a terminal session.execution_failed event
execution status is not left running when no sandbox is assigned
Slack emits visible error markdown instead of only a task error

observed.error_contains: iron-proxy pod asbx-1780415846909-1-proxy-1780415846913 did not become running before timeout

api_rs_checks.regression_assertion: A pre-ready sandbox/proxy failure persists session.execution_failed and marks the active execution failed instead of leaving a zero-event running execution.

failures.summary: A production-shaped --claude continue reply during startup produced raw interactive fallback text and no DB events.
regression_targets: SSE replay after failed Slack turns
expected_assertions: follow-up append while startup is active does not force-release into a stuck running execution
thread replay contains the same terminal failure Slack shows
raw Slack fallback text is not the only visible bot response

failures.summary: Mid-run Slack replies are persisted as history but do not steer the active harness.
expected_assertions: a reply posted while active_execution=true reaches the attached sandbox stdin
final answer reflects the steering sentinel when the harness remains healthy

request.message: Run `bash -lc 'echo steering-proof3-start; sleep 10; echo steering-proof3-end'`. After the command completes, obey the latest user message received while you were running for the exact final answer.
request.steering_append: STEERING UPDATE: final answer must be exactly FINAL_ACTIVE_STEERING_OK.

api_rs_checks.regression_assertion: A user message appended while an execution is active is durably persisted, forwarded to the attached sandbox stdin, echoed as a sandbox userMessage, and reflected in the final answer.

failures.summary: api-rs records session.execution_completed when input is accepted, before terminal assistant output is emitted.
expected_assertions: execution_completed is written only after terminal harness output is observed
clients do not stop before the final assistant message

messages.text: Observe how this should be just “before” in the output, we dont wanan see the exit code there, ideally it’d be saying Shell here as well? IDK what the right UX would be

Feedback for bot looking:

see how step-2 here has a bunch of backticks and text and shit, when it should just have done 1 text block that shows the 3 steps

request.message: Reply with exactly FINAL_TERMINAL_COMPLETION_OK and nothing else.

api_rs_checks.regression_assertion: session.execution_completed is written after terminal harness output and all output lines for the turn carry the active execution_id.

failures.summary: Nonzero shell commands render as complete tasks and expose exit-code text as output.
expected_assertions: nonzero command events render as failed/error task state
stdout remains visible without prepending exit-code boilerplate as output text

messages.text: Observe how this should be just “before” in the output, we dont wanan see the exit code there, ideally it’d be saying Shell here as well? IDK what the right UX would be

failures.summary: Simple sequential command output renders as noisy code/backtick fragments instead of one clean text block.
expected_assertions: stdout for step-1/step-2/step-3 is rendered as one clean text block
task details do not duplicate code fences around simple stdout

messages.text:
Feedback for bot looking:

see how step-2 here has a bunch of backticks and text and shit, when it should just have done 1 text block that shows the 3 steps

failures.summary: max_duration_ms is accepted but a 3s request can run 12s and complete.
expected_assertions: execution is cancelled or failed when max_duration_ms elapses
timeout terminal state is persisted and replayable

request.message: Run `bash -lc 'echo timeout-proof2-start; sleep 12; echo timeout-proof2-end'`, then answer exactly FINAL_TIMEOUT2_SHOULD_NOT_APPEAR.

api_rs_checks.regression_assertion: max_duration_ms produces a durable terminal session.execution_failed event, keeps the execution failed, and suppresses late sandbox output after the timeout failure.

failures.summary: Concurrent execute calls for one thread leak a DB unique-constraint error as HTTP 500.
expected_assertions: second active execute returns an intentional conflict/queued response
raw database constraint text is never exposed to clients

response_body.error: session slack:C0APUQ8U5T9:1780412340.324149 already has an active execution

api_rs_checks.regression_assertion: A second active execute returns an intentional conflict and does not leak a raw database constraint error or create another active execution.

failures.summary: Slackbot parser can stop on malformed/plain output or early terminal markers before later final answer deltas.
expected_assertions: plain output lines do not stop consumption before a later terminal answer
turn.completed does not cause the final answer delta to be dropped

synthetic_cases.expected_regression_assertion: Plain output should not stop stream consumption before a later terminal answer.
Renderer should not report final completion before final-answer deltas are consumed.

failures.summary: Final-looking answer text can be lost or rendered only inside a Thinking task.
expected_assertions: nested terminal result text is normalized into visible markdown_text
answer text is never present only inside task details

synthetic_cases.expected_regression_assertion: Slack stream should contain visible final text or renderer should normalize nested terminal result text.
Thinking task must not be the only visible location for final-answer content.

failures.summary: Renderer produces an error close event, but Slack-visible chunks have no markdown error text.
expected_assertions: execution failure emits visible final markdown error text
task error state is supplementary, not the only user-visible response

synthetic_cases.expected_regression_assertion: Slack should include visible error text, not only an error-state plan task.

failures.summary: Idle/retry production threads later receive internal 'no rollout found' messages. Likely tied to stale runtime state or future pause/resume behavior.
expected_assertions: reply after idle resumes or recreates from durable thread history
Slack never exposes 'no rollout found for thread id'
resume failures persist terminal session.execution_failed events

failures.summary: After an idle timeout pause, api-rs resumes the sandbox but the next Codex app-server turn can fail before producing a final answer.
regression_targets: SSE replay of failed resume turns
expected_assertions: reply after idle pause produces a final answer or recreates from durable history
resume emits a durable healthy terminal state, not only startup/user events then turn.failed
Slack does not show a thinking-only error block for resume failures

request.message: Reply with exactly FINAL_IDLE_RESUME_OK and nothing else.

observed.harness_error_details: timeout waiting for child process to exit

api_rs_checks.regression_assertion: A reply after an idle pause must resume or recreate the sandbox and produce a final answer from durable thread history; it must not fail after only startup/user-message events.

failures.summary: A reply in a subscribed Slack thread after the session is idle is appended to history but does not execute a new turn or produce a bot response.
expected_assertions: idle subscribed-thread replies either execute a new turn or receive an explicit visible response
Slackbot does not silently append an idle user reply with open_stream=false
DB and Slack readback agree on whether a follow-up produced an execution

slack_messages.reply_text: idle pause Slack repro follow-up after pause. Reply with exactly SLACK_IDLE_RESUME2_OK and nothing else.

slack_checks.regression_assertion: A subscribed Slack thread reply while the session is idle should either execute a new turn or make the non-execution policy explicit; it must not silently append with no bot response.

failures.summary: Production seed shows an empty bot reply followed by Request cancelled after a user reports blank response.
expected_assertions: empty assistant messages are not posted as final Slack replies
Request cancelled is only emitted for explicit cancel or durable cancellation

failures.summary: Production seeds contain repeated pre-execution failure messages, runtime issues, and stream-without-assistant errors.
expected_assertions: pre-execution failures are durable terminal states
retries do not duplicate identical failure messages in one thread
